channel.title: jvm on Jorge Branch's Tech Blog
channel.description: Recent content in jvm on Jorge Branch's Tech Blog

item.title: A ThreadPoolExecutor Deep-Dive
item.description: Introduction Java has been designed from its inception with multi-threading in mind. Unfortunately, and although all the needed concurrency primitives were from the very beginning made available to developers, development could quickly get unwildy: a lot of boilerplate code had to be recreated time and time again across applications, and that was quite often not even the biggest hurdle – it’s no secret that getting concurrent algorithms right is hard.

item.title: Implementing a Promise in Scala
item.description: Introduction Frequently in concurrent programming, the problem of wanting to Frequently in concurrent programming, one may need to pass a placeholder object to another part of the application and be notified when The Scala libraries provide two related ways to deal with the computation of /** * A thread-safe container for a value that can be set by calls to `set()` * and retrieved by calls to `get()`. Any of those methods can be invoked * by any thread.
